children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Commissioner, in consultation with the Ticket to Work and Work Incentives Advisory Panel established under section 101(f) of the Ticket to Work and Work Incentives Improvement Act of 1999, shall establish a community-based work incentives planning and assistance program for the purpose of disseminating accurate information to disabled beneficiaries on work incentives programs and issues related to such programs.

children.heading: Grants, cooperative agreements, contracts, and outreach
children.label: 2 Grants, cooperative agreements, contracts, and outreach
A
children.text: establish a competitive program of grants, cooperative agreements, or contracts to provide benefits planning and assistance, including information on the availability of protection and advocacy services, to disabled beneficiaries, including individuals participating in the Ticket to Work and Self-Sufficiency Program established under , the program established under , and other programs that are designed to encourage disabled beneficiaries to work;section 1320b–19 of this titlesection 1382h of this title

children.label: i
children.text: preparing and disseminating information explaining such programs; and

children.label: ii
children.text: working in cooperation with other Federal, State, and private agencies and nonprofit organizations that serve disabled beneficiaries, and with agencies and organizations that focus on vocational rehabilitation and work-related training and counseling;

children.label: i
children.text: disabled beneficiaries;

children.label: ii
children.text: benefit applicants under subchapters II and XVI; and

children.label: iii
children.text: individuals or entities awarded grants under subparagraphs (A) or (B); and1So in original. Probably should be “subparagraph”.1

children.label: i
children.text: training for work incentives specialists and individuals providing planning assistance described in subparagraph (C); and

children.label: ii
children.text: technical assistance to organizations and entities that are designed to encourage disabled beneficiaries to return to work.

children.heading: Coordination with other programs
children.label: 3 Coordination with other programs
children.text: The responsibilities of the Commissioner established under this section shall be coordinated with other public and private programs that provide information and assistance regarding rehabilitation services and independent living supports and benefits planning for disabled beneficiaries including the program under , the plans for achieving self-support program (PASS), and any other Federal or State work incentives programs that are designed to assist disabled beneficiaries, including educational agencies that provide information and assistance regarding rehabilitation, school-to-work programs, transition services (as defined in, and provided in accordance with, the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act ( et seq.)), a one-stop delivery system established under , and other services.section 1382h of this title20 U.S.C. 1400section 3151(e) of title 29

children.heading: Application
children.label: A Application
children.text: An entity shall submit an application for a grant, cooperative agreement, or contract to provide benefits planning and assistance to the Commissioner at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Commissioner may determine is necessary to meet the requirements of this section.

children.heading: Statewideness
children.label: B Statewideness
children.text: The Commissioner shall ensure that the planning, assistance, and information described in paragraph (2) shall be available on a statewide basis.

children.heading: Eligibility of States and private organizations
children.label: C Eligibility of States and private organizations

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Commissioner may award a grant, cooperative agreement, or contract under this section to a State or a private agency or organization (other than Social Security Administration Field Offices and the State agency administering the State medicaid program under subchapter XIX, including any agency or entity described in clause (ii), that the Commissioner determines is qualified to provide the planning, assistance, and information described in paragraph (2)).

children.label: I
children.text: Any public or private agency or organization (including Centers for Independent Living established under title VII of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 ( et seq.), protection and advocacy organizations, client assistance programs established in accordance with section 112 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 (), and State Developmental Disabilities Councils established in accordance with section 6024 of this title) that the Commissioner determines satisfies the requirements of this section.29 U.S.C. 79629 U.S.C. 7322See References in Text note below.2

children.label: II
children.text: The State agency administering the State program funded under part A of subchapter IV.

children.heading: Exclusion for conflict of interest
children.label: D Exclusion for conflict of interest
children.text: The Commissioner may not award a grant, cooperative agreement, or contract under this section to any entity that the Commissioner determines would have a conflict of interest if the entity were to receive a grant, cooperative agreement, or contract under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: availability and interrelation of any Federal or State work incentives programs designed to assist disabled beneficiaries that the individual may be eligible to participate in;

children.label: B
children.text: adequacy of any health benefits coverage that may be offered by an employer of the individual and the extent to which other health benefits coverage may be available to the individual; and

children.label: C
children.text: availability of protection and advocacy services for disabled beneficiaries and how to access such services.

children.heading: Amount of grants, cooperative agreements, or contracts
children.label: 3 Amount of grants, cooperative agreements, or contracts

children.heading: Based on population of disabled beneficiaries
children.label: A Based on population of disabled beneficiaries
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the Commissioner shall award a grant, cooperative agreement, or contract under this section to an entity based on the percentage of the population of the State where the entity is located who are disabled beneficiaries.

children.heading: Per grant
children.label: i Per grant
children.text: No entity shall receive a grant, cooperative agreement, or contract under this section for a fiscal year that is less than $50,000 or more than $300,000.

children.heading: Total amount for all grants, cooperative agreements, and contracts
children.label: ii Total amount for all grants, cooperative agreements, and contracts
children.text: The total amount of all grants, cooperative agreements, and contracts awarded under this section for a fiscal year may not exceed $23,000,000.

children.heading: Allocation of costs
children.label: A Allocation of costs
children.text: The costs of carrying out this section shall be paid from amounts made available for the administration of subchapter II and amounts made available for the administration of subchapter XVI, and shall be allocated among those amounts as appropriate.

children.heading: Carryover
children.label: B Carryover
children.text: An amount not in excess of 10 percent of the total amount obligated through a grant, cooperative agreement, or contract awarded under this section for a fiscal year to a State or a private agency or organization shall remain available for obligation to such State or private agency or organization until the end of the succeeding fiscal year. Any such amount remaining available for obligation during such succeeding fiscal year shall be available for providing benefits planning and assistance only for individuals who are within the caseload of the recipient of the grant, agreement, or contract as of immediately before the beginning of such fiscal year.

children.heading: Annual report
children.label: c Annual report
children.text: Each entity awarded a grant, cooperative agreement, or contract under this section shall submit an annual report to the Commissioner on the benefits planning and assistance provided to individuals under such grant, agreement, or contract.

children.heading: Commissioner
children.label: 1 Commissioner
children.text: The term “Commissioner” means the Commissioner of Social Security.

children.label: A
children.text: who is a disabled beneficiary as defined in ;section 1320b–19(k)(2) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: who is receiving a cash payment described in or a supplementary payment described in (without regard to whether such payment is paid by the Commissioner pursuant to an agreement under or under );section 1382e(a) of this titlesection 212(a)(3) of Public Law 93–66section 1382e(a) of this titlesection 212(b) of Public Law 93–66

children.label: C
children.text: who, pursuant to , is considered to be receiving benefits under subchapter XVI of this chapter; orsection 1382h(b) of this title

children.label: D
children.text: who is entitled to benefits under part A of subchapter XVIII of this chapter by reason of the penultimate sentence of .section 426(b) of this title

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: e Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $23,000,000 for each of the fiscal years 2000 through 2011.
